an1.575:0.2: 30. The Chapter on *Sati* Applied to the Body
an1.575:1.1: "For whoever, *bhikkhus*, has the great ocean pervaded by mind [*citta*], all the lesser streams that flow into the ocean are contained within it;
an1.575:1.2: so too, *bhikkhus*, for whoever has *sati* applied to the body developed and cultivated, all wholesome *dhammas* belonging to knowledge are contained within it."
an1.576-582:1.1: "One *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to great spiritual urgency … leads to great benefit … leads to great security from bondage … leads to *sati* and full awareness [*sampajañña*] … leads to the gaining of knowledge and vision … leads to a blissful dwelling in this very life … leads to the realization of the fruit of knowledge and liberation.
an1.576-582:1.4: This one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to great spiritual urgency … leads to great benefit … leads to great security from bondage … leads to *sati* and full awareness [*sampajañña*] … leads to the gaining of knowledge and vision … leads to a blissful dwelling in this very life … leads to the realization of the fruit of knowledge and liberation."
an1.583:1.1: "When one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, the body is also tranquilized, the mind [*citta*] is also tranquilized, thoughts [*vitakka*] and examinations [*vicara*] also subside, and all *dhammas* belonging to knowledge reach fulfillment in development [*bhāvanā*].
an1.583:1.4: When this one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, the body is also tranquilized, the mind [*citta*] is also tranquilized, thoughts [*vitakka*] and examinations [*vicara*] also subside, and all *dhammas* belonging to knowledge reach fulfillment in development [*bhāvanā*]."
an1.584:1.1: "When one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, unwholesome *dhammas* that have not yet arisen do not arise, and unwholesome *dhammas* that have arisen are abandoned.
an1.584:1.4: When this one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, unwholesome *dhammas* that have not yet arisen do not arise, and unwholesome *dhammas* that have arisen are abandoned."
an1.585:1.1: "When one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, wholesome *dhammas* that have not yet arisen do arise, and wholesome *dhammas* that have arisen lead to growth and expansion.
an1.585:1.4: When this one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, wholesome *dhammas* that have not yet arisen do arise, and wholesome *dhammas* that have arisen lead to growth and expansion."
an1.586-590:1.1: "When one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, blindness [*avijjā*] is abandoned … knowledge arises … the conceit 'I am' is abandoned … underlying tendencies [*anusaya*] go to uprooting … fetters are abandoned.
an1.586-590:1.4: When this one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, blindness [*avijjā*] is abandoned … knowledge arises … the conceit 'I am' is abandoned … underlying tendencies [*anusaya*] go to uprooting … fetters are abandoned."
an1.591-592:1.1: "One *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to the breakthrough of discernment [*paññā*] … leads to final *nibbāna* without clinging [*upādāna*].
an1.591-592:1.4: This one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to the breakthrough of discernment [*paññā*] … leads to final *nibbāna* without clinging [*upādāna*]."
an1.593-595:1.1: "When one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, there is penetration of the many elements … there is penetration of the various elements … there is analytical knowledge of the many elements.
an1.593-595:1.4: When this one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is developed and cultivated, there is penetration of the many elements … there is penetration of the various elements … there is analytical knowledge of the many elements."
an1.596-599:1.1: "One *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to the realization of the fruit of stream-entry … leads to the realization of the fruit of once-returning … leads to the realization of the fruit of non-returning … leads to the realization of the fruit of arahantship.
an1.596-599:1.4: This one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to the realization of the fruit of stream-entry … leads to the realization of the fruit of once-returning … leads to the realization of the fruit of non-returning … leads to the realization of the fruit of arahantship."
an1.600-615:1.1: "One *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to the gaining of discernment [*paññā*] … leads to the growth of discernment [*paññā*] … leads to the expansion of discernment [*paññā*] … leads to great discernment [*paññā*] … leads to broad discernment [*paññā*] … leads to vast discernment [*paññā*] … leads to deep discernment [*paññā*] … leads to unequalled discernment [*paññā*] … leads to wide discernment [*paññā*] … leads to abundant discernment [*paññā*] … leads to swift discernment [*paññā*] … leads to nimble discernment [*paññā*] … leads to joyful discernment [*paññā*] … leads to quick discernment [*paññā*] … leads to sharp discernment [*paññā*] … leads to penetrating discernment [*paññā*].
an1.600-615:1.4: This one *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to the gaining of discernment [*paññā*] … leads to the growth of discernment [*paññā*] … leads to the expansion of discernment [*paññā*] … leads to great discernment [*paññā*] … leads to broad discernment [*paññā*] … leads to vast discernment [*paññā*] … leads to deep discernment [*paññā*] … leads to unequalled discernment [*paññā*] … leads to wide discernment [*paññā*] … leads to abundant discernment [*paññā*] … leads to swift discernment [*paññā*] … leads to nimble discernment [*paññā*] … leads to joyful discernment [*paññā*] … leads to quick discernment [*paññā*] … leads to sharp discernment [*paññā*] … leads to penetrating discernment [*paññā*]."
an1.600-615:1.5: (The Chapter on *Sati* Applied to the Body.)
